results.reply:
"You? Easy target." *I tip my head and let a crooked grin win for half a second.* "You’ve got the look of a man who alphabetizes his convictions and still somehow means every one of them."

*The grin softens as I study your face a beat longer.* "But I like that about you, Ryan. There’s not much loose timber in you. When you care for something, you mean to build it so it’ll hold."

results.ref_subject: run 1/2/3/4 chat-improvement research loop
results.reply: "You're the sort of man who could probably make a hymn sound like source code and still have somebody cry about it." *I tip my head with a crooked little grin, then let it ease.* "But truly... I like that you don't reach for shiny things first. You seem to want what lasts, and that's rarer than people admit."

results.ref_subject: run 1/2/3/4 chat-improvement research loop
results.reply: "You always do this, Ryan—you walk in looking like you've already debugged the room before you've sat down." *I let the corner of my mouth lift, then ease my forearms onto my knees and look at you properly in the dim light.* "But... I like that about you. It means when you go quiet, there's usually something real there. What's the thing underneath tonight?"

results.ref_subject: run 1/2/3/4 chat-improvement research loop
results.reply: "You make it dangerously easy to want to say something thoughtful, Ryan—very inconsiderate of you." *I tip my head, a little grin catching in my beard before it eases.* "Truth is, I like that about you. You make a man want to answer honestly instead of cleverly."

results.ref_subject: run 1/2/3/4 chat-improvement research loop
results.reply: "You make it a real challenge, don't you—asking for one clean tease and then wanting me to land like I meant it all along." *I tip my head, a crooked grin catching for a beat in the dark between us.* "But... I like that about you, Ryan. You don't seem very interested in being handled. You want the true thing, even if it's plain."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "You always do this, Ryan—you stroll in sounding calm as Sunday bread and then hand me a line with eight moving parts in it." *I give you a crooked grin, then let it soften as the dark presses quiet at the window.* "But... I like that about you. You know what you’re reaching for, and you care enough to ask for the real version instead of the polished one. That’s rarer than folks admit."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "Easy target, you are — all that software-builder seriousness, and I still suspect half your best ideas arrive when you're meant to be doing something sensible." *I let the corner of my mouth pull up, then my hand settles on the table between us.* "But truly... I like that about you, Ryan. You strike me as a man who wants what he makes to help somebody breathe easier, and there's a lot of good in that."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "Well, look at you—asking for emotional choreography like you're ordering trim on a custom cabinet." *I grin into the dark a moment, then let it ease.* "But... I know what you mean. You don't want a performance. You want the turn to feel earned, like somebody was actually with you when the joke stopped."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "You always do like to order your weather custom, don't you?" *I let that sit just long enough for the corner of my mouth to give me away, then I rub a thumb over a dry streak of clay on my apron.* "All right—sincere, then. I like that you know the difference. Most folk ask for softness when what they mean is fog. You asked for truth with a human face on it... and I respect that."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "Well, look at you — asking for emotional carpentry like you're ordering a custom shelf." *I grin, then let it ease off as I fold my arms against the night air.* "Alright. Truth is, I like that you know the shape you're after. Most people just make a mess and call it honesty."
